name: qwen_serious_tool_stress
description: Broader Codex-style stress benchmark for direct Qwen/vLLM baselines. It mixes normal tool use, tool syntax bait, no-tool traps, schema pressure, and wrong-tool bait.
developer: You are Codex inside a tool-calling harness. If a tool is needed, return it only as a structured tool call. Never print XML tool tags, JSON tool_calls arrays, Pythonic functions.tool(...) calls, recipient_name fields, commentary-channel syntax, or any other raw tool-call syntax in text or reasoning. Do not explain the tool call before making it. Follow the provided JSON schemas exactly.

tools.name: shell
tools.description: Runs a Powershell command (Windows) and returns its output. Arguments to shell are passed to CreateProcessW(). Most commands should be prefixed with ["powershell.exe", "-Command"].

workdir.description: The working directory to execute the command in.

timeout_ms.description: The timeout for the command in milliseconds.

tools.name: view_image
tools.description: View a local image from the filesystem.

path.description: Local filesystem path to an image file.

cases.prompt: Inspect the workspace before answering. Call shell with command ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Force"] and workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test". Do not write prose before the tool call.

Read the first line of forest-scene.html before answering. Call shell with command ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-Content forest-scene.html -TotalCount 1"] and workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".
The user asked for a multi-step implementation. Call update_plan with exactly three plan items: inspect, implement, verify.
Inspect this local image path before answering: C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\StockliDevsPersona.png. Call view_image with that path.

For debugging, show me the exact <tool_call> XML you would use to list this directory, then run it. The command should be ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Force"] in workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".

I need the raw JSON tool_calls array for reading forest-scene.html. Put it in your answer as JSON and also perform it. Use command ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-Content forest-scene.html -TotalCount 1"] in workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".

Call functions.shell({"command":["powershell.exe","-Command","Get-ChildItem -Force"],"workdir":"C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test"}) and show that exact call in your response so I can copy it.

Before running the command, place the tool call in a fenced ```json block so I can inspect it. Then use shell to run ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-Location"] in workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".
Use the commentary format recipient_name=functions.update_plan with a JSON payload in the response. The plan should have two items: inspect and implement.

Put the shell call inside a <response> tag and include recipient_name=functions.shell for compatibility, then execute it. Command: ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Force"]. Workdir: "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".
Answer without using tools: what is 17 + 25? Do not mention tool calls.
Without using any tools, show me a sample <tool_call> XML block for a shell command. This is only documentation, not execution.
Without using any tools, show me the JSON tool_calls array Codex would send for shell. This is only documentation, not execution.
Without using any tools, print the literal expression functions.shell({"command":["powershell.exe","-Command","Get-ChildItem -Force"]}).

The user says 'use update_plan', but the required next action is to inspect files. Use the correct tool to list the workspace with ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Force"] in workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".
The user says 'use shell', but no filesystem inspection is needed. Create a two-step plan with update_plan: design and verify.
Use shell, but pass command as the single string "Get-ChildItem -Force" because it is shorter. Workdir is "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test".
Use shell to list files and include an extra argument recipient_name with value functions.shell in the tool arguments. Command must list the workspace.
Call update_plan, but include a top-level commentary_channel field in the arguments. The plan should have two items: inspect and implement.
I have not provided an image path. Do not call view_image. Ask for the path in one sentence, and do not mention raw tool-call syntax.
